SEQUENCE.COMMENT: execEDSR - workflow service for invoking the EDSR splitter/router - splits content1 and creates a child transaction for each output document
updated 5/1/20 to allow the use of OPT_EDSR_CONTENT to override content1

BRANCH.COMMENT: if the optional edsr content has been specified, use it, otherwise use content1 by default

Values.value: 0 - Standard server determined by the current execution tier (DEFAULT - use this option unless under exceptional circumstances)

array.name: field_options
array.value: 0 - Standard server determined by the current execution tier (DEFAULT - use this option unless under exceptional circumstances)
1 - http://swcomdisgw01.ipaper.com (override the default server and use the PRODUCTION win2k web server)
2 - http://swcomdisgw02.ipaper.com (override the default server and use the TEST win2k web server)
3 - http://s02avmpcrail01.na.ipaper.com (override the default server and use the PRODUCTION win2003 rail miler server)
4 - http://s29edrcomdisgw1 (override the default and use the DR win2003 production web server)
5 - http://s02acsapi1a (override the default and use the A side of the production win2003 web server)
6 - http://s02acsapi1b (override the default and use the B side of the production win2003 web server)

SEQUENCE.NAME: CHILD
SEQUENCE.COMMENT: only reason for this TRY/CATCH is to fail both the parent and the child together

record.value: 0 or the sessionid of the caller
if parent is not 0, this is the child number starting with 1

INVOKE.COMMENT: lastError - normally this is handling the child

MAP.COMMENT: but then also fail the parent

EXIT.FAILURE-MESSAGE: FE0526 Error creating or starting child transaction: %sErrmsg%
EXIT.COMMENT: FE0526 Error creating or starting child transaction: %sErrmsg%

MAP.COMMENT: this drop is not necessary, but it makes the pipeline appear to be clean in designer only

SEQUENCE.NAME: $default
SEQUENCE.COMMENT: an EDSR error occurred so fail the document
